commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `openclaw/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows`, rewires live transport scenario planning through it, and adds QA Lab `qa user-flows` list/plan/run commands with docs, export metadata, and tests.

PR surface: Source +1012, Tests +330, Docs +1, Config +4, Generated 0, Other +4. Total +1351 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a QA planning and plugin SDK capability rather than reporting a current-main bug. The claimed behavior is covered by source-checkout CLI/tests in the PR body, but live transport behavior was not exercised.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the blocker is a public SDK/API contract decision plus conflict refresh, not a narrow autonomous code defect.

target.title: Add live transport scenario planning
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `openclaw/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows`, a shared user-flow catalog/planner with surfaces, capability atoms, optional owner mappings, running-gateway execution metadata, and selected/skipped plan output.
- Reworks live transport standard scenarios to sit on top of the generic user-flow planner and maps channel cases like canary, mention gating, allowlist block, threads, reactions, restart, and help to user-flow metadata.
- Adds `qa user-flows list`, `qa user-flows plan`, and `qa user-flows run` to QA Lab. `run` compiles selected `qa-lab-flow` user flows into existing QA Suite scenario ids and executes the existing suite runner; it does not introduce a JSON step executor.
- Exposes the planner through QA runtime, package exports, SDK docs metadata, and the plugin SDK API baseline so QA Lab/Kova can build on the same contract instead of inventing a parallel JSON language.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-live-transport-scenarios.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/user-flow-cli.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts --threads=8`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw now has a shared user-flow planning contract and a QA CLI execution path that can select runnable flows, explain skipped flows, and run selected QA Lab-backed flows through the existing QA Suite runner.
Real environment tested: Local source checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-user-flows`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxlint src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-live-transport-scenarios.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/user-flow-cli.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts --threads=8`; `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`; `pnpm lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: The planner tests pass for optional mappings, requested-flow filtering, live-transport-backed flows, unknown id validation, selected/skipped output, and the QA CLI tests prove `qa user-flows run` forwards selected user flows to existing QA Suite scenario ids.
Observed result after fix: User-flow plans carry running-gateway execution metadata and report selected flows plus explicit skip reasons; executable QA Lab-backed flows run via existing QA Suite command plumbing.
What was not tested: No live Telegram/Slack/etc. lane was run. The CLI is currently under the existing private/source-checkout QA Lab command surface; making this available in ordinary packaged OpenClaw releases would require intentionally shipping or exposing QA Lab/scenario runner assets that are currently excluded from npm packages.
target.author: romneyda
